name: Vegan Roast
recipeIngredient: 2 cups Chick peas, cooked and mashed
About 3/4 cup Dry (may substitute pinto, kidney, or adzuki beans)
1 1/2 cups Parsnips, 1/4-inch pieces OR 1 1/2 cups Turnips, white, small (cut into 1/4-inch pieces)
Up to 3 Tamari soy sauce (up to 3 tablespoons)
1 1/2 cups Nuts or seeds, partially ground, up to 2 cups (raw pecans, almonds, filberts, or sunflower seeds)
3/4 cup Parsley, chopped, up to 1 cup
3/4 cup Buckwheat flour or amaranth flour
1/4 cup Arrowroot powder or soy flour or chick pea flour
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the chickpeas overnight and cook until tender.
2. Steam the carrots, celery, and parsnips (or turnips) until tender.
3. Mash the chickpeas and vegetables with the bouillon and other flavorings (cinnamon, soy sauce, sea salt, sea kelp, and cayenne pepper) until thoroughly mixed and smooth.
4. Add the raw onion and all remaining ingredients; mix everything well using your hands.
5. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
6. Oil a 9" x 13-inch baking dish and line it with wax paper, then oil the wax paper.
7. Shape the mixture into an oblong loaf in the pan with rounded top and edges.
8. Bake for 80-90 minutes until well browned and cooked to your liking. The edges easily remove from the wax paper and can be placed on a platter on a bed of greens and garnished with peeled tomato flowers or radish roses. A thin coating of flax oil or other fresh, natural oil may be brushed on top for a glistening finish.
9. Serves 4-6
